mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which correlates with short-term equity market optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows, or 5-day returns are negative in >55% of rising INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive policy creates stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0 and FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 150,000 signals robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payrolls above 150k trigger negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across macro and micro levels, reinforcing earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Combined positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Contracting industrial output alongside rising unemployment indicates a supply-demand mismatch and recessionary onset, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances.
